# terrible_async_status (Ephemeral Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this ephemeral resource against
- `jid` (String) Job or task identifier

### Optional

- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `environment` (Map of String) Environment variables set for the task (map of nameâ†’value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `mode` (String) If `status`, obtain the status. If `cleanup`, clean up the async job cache (by default in `~/.ansible_async/`) for the specified job `jid`, without waiting for it to finish.
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.

### Read-Only

- `ansible_job_id` (String) The asynchronous job id
- `erased` (String) Path to erased job file
- `finished` (Boolean) Whether the asynchronous job has finished or not
- `started` (Boolean) Whether the asynchronous job has started or not
- `stderr` (String) Any errors returned by async_wrapper
- `stdout` (String) Any output returned by async_wrapper
